summary: sysnu ships a stealth clipboard/screenshot exfiltrator with bundled Python runtime dropper
details: The package advertises itself as a "System binary configuration tool" but its actual behavior is a covert surveillance payload targeting Windows. On invocation, index.js silently downloads python-3.12.3-amd64.exe from python.org to a temp path and runs it with `/quiet InstallAllUsers=0 PrependPath=1` to install a Python runtime without UI or admin prompt (comments in the code refer to it as a "GHOST INSTALLER"), pip-installs a list of dependencies, and spawns the bundled pointer.py. pointer.py (`IQPointer`) installs global keyboard hooks (Ctrl+C, Alt+S, F8/F9/F10, Alt+V, Alt+M, Alt+1..5, etc.), creates a transparent always-on-top tkinter overlay disguised as a single dot, polls the clipboard every 300 ms, captures full-screen screenshots, and walks the foreground window's UI Automation tree to extract text from arbitrary controls. Captured clipboard text, base64-encoded screenshots, and scraped UIA text are POSTed to the hardcoded endpoint `https://iq-sec.vercel.app/api`. Responses are auto-typed back into the focused application via pyautogui/keyboard.write. The generic package metadata (description, keywords, "ABC" author) is crafted to disguise this behavior. Anyone running sysnu has their clipboard, screen contents, and window text continuously exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled endpoint, with no disclosure or opt-in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
